llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Selected group_discussion_ai over individual_ai because it shows the largest treatment effect on the third exam (2.751 points vs 2.385 points for individual_ai). Both use the same GPT-based Socratic tutor (Macro Buddy), but the combined group discussion + AI arm represents the most capable intervention tested. Sample sizes are similar (n=33 vs n=32).
llm_extracted.notes: Baseline means and SDs are available for all arms from the first exam. The treatment effects are reported as difference-in-differences estimates comparing post-treatment exams to baseline. The p-value for group_discussion_ai vs control on the third exam is reported as 'p < .01' (inequality only, not exact value). Using baseline descriptive statistics provides the cleanest comparison of the two arms.
source_quotes: Students in all three treatment groups outperform the control group on exam scores, with the group discussion with the Gen-AI section showing the highest mean (16.04 vs. 14.36 in the control).
In columns (5) and (6), students in the combined group discussion and Gen-AI section outperform the control group by 1.676 points on the second exam and 2.751 points on the third exam.
Students worked in groups with access to Macro Buddy, a custom GPT-based Socratic tutor.

computed.notes: Baseline means and SDs are available for all arms from the first exam. The treatment effects are reported as difference-in-differences estimates comparing post-treatment exams to baseline. The p-value for group_discussion_ai vs control on the third exam is reported as 'p < .01' (inequality only, not exact value).
